topic: Areas of Inquiry: Music for Listening and Performance
subject: music

items.q: How many crotchets are in a semibreve?
items.why: Students confuse the relative durations. A semibreve (whole note) = 2 minims = 4 crotchets = 8 quavers. Each level halves in length.

items.q: How does D Dorian differ from D minor?
items.correct: Dorian has a raised 6th (B natural) compared to natural minor.
items.random_wrong: Dorian has no sharps or flats at all.
items.why: Students treat modes as variants of major/minor. Dorian on D uses only white keys (like C major, but starting on D), giving a raised 6th compared to D natural minor - producing the modal flavour.
Students confuse andante (walking, moderate) with adagio (slow). Andante literally means 'going/walking' in Italian - it's a comfortable pace, not slow.

items.q: Whose 1953 Concentus Musicus Wien revived historically informed Baroque playing?
items.why: It's Nikolaus Harnoncourt. “John Eliot Gardiner” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
Correct answer: Christopher Small. “Susan McClary” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year did Glenn Gould abandon live performance?
items.why: The real figure is 1964 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
